what_it_does: Root React component for the Surface application. It detects viewport form factor, maintains a large shared projection state, fetches and live-tails a semantic projection, dispatches selection and V-verb events, and renders the active layout plus a stack of overlay faces (gallery, sessions, channels, board, transcript, tools, decisions, source panel, and the right-hand assistant).
observations: Classifies viewport into desktop/portrait/landscape using a 1024px width threshold and orientation, not just media queries.
Maintains a single SurfaceState object passed into Desktop/Portrait/Landscape layout components.
Fetches /api/projection with a parameter map built from binding, nucleation, centre, separator poles, time scrub, rung, embedder, dim, quant, and space.
Uses an EventSource to /api/stream for live updates and throttles re-fetches to ~2.5 seconds.

Emits a CustomEvent 'projection:select' with both a content-resolvable address and a canonical source address, deliberately using an empty address for activity events so consumers can distinguish them from deselect.
Listens to 'gallery:verb' and routes navigate, open-source, drive, generate, annotate/decision_take, and accept-update verbs, with verify-mode guards that suppress writes.
Persists the chosen lens binding in localStorage and degrades to 'raw' if the persisted lens no longer exists.
Sets document.body.dataset.ff and calls resolveAndApplyModal to coordinate modal allocation with CSS.

Renders many sibling overlay components regardless of layout: SurfaceNav, GalleryMount, DecisionsInbox, SessionDrill, ChannelView, BoardView, TranscriptView, ToolsBar/ToolsPanel, RightHand, SourcePanel.
standouts: The file is a central orchestrator: state, data fetching, event bus dispatch, and face hosting all live in one component.
The 'projection:select' handoff includes detailed comments about address semantics and a unit-key guard to avoid re-firing on every re-projection.
Verify-mode guards are wired directly into decision_take and accept-update handlers, with loud notices and console logs.
The V verb dispatcher is implemented as a single window event listener that branches on verb name.
conventions: React functional components with hooks (useState, useEffect, useCallback, useRef).
TypeScript type aliases exported for shared state shapes.
Window-level CustomEvent bus for cross-component communication (projection:select, gallery:verb, gallery:rerender).
localStorage for operator continuity where staleness is later validated.
Inline essay-style comments that cite design seed sections, dates, and seam contracts.
Effect-driven data fetching with an 'alive' flag to ignore stale responses.
concerns: The component accumulates many responsibilities and state fields, making it a large integration surface.
Several nested if/else branches in onVerb are long and could be hard to extend safely.
The fallback fetch('/api/territory/write') inside onVerb duplicates logic that normally goes through window.forkBrainCore.writeDirections.
Event listeners for resize/orientationchange are registered in multiple effects with separate cleanup paths.
The 'core' helper is typed with an inline cast rather than a declared interface.
Hardcoded pixel threshold (1024) and throttle duration (2500ms) are buried in the implementation.
notes: This is the Surface app's root state machine and event switchboard: it wires the canvas, the V, the gallery, and the breadth faces together around a live projection.
questions: What is the full contract for /api/projection parameters and how are unknown params handled server-side?
Which components actually consume 'projection:select' and how do they behave when address is empty?
Is window.forkBrainCore always present in production, or is the fetch fallback the normal path?
How is the verify mode flag surfaced to non-React consumers or server-side routes?
What happens if multiple overlays (GalleryMount, SessionDrill, ChannelView, etc.) are triggered simultaneously?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose as a live, multi-face projection surface matches the implementation; it fetches, tails, selects, and hosts the expected faces.
apparent_themes: Verification discipline and non-contamination of real decisions
intention_signals: The file is trying to be a single source of truth for 'what the operator is looking at right now' and a neutral switchboard that lets the canvas, the V, and various drill-in faces all agree on the selected unit and its address semantics.
novelty: The dense inline seam narrative that maps every block to a design seed clause or a runtime contract, plus the explicit handling of 'addressless' activity events in the selection handoff.
contribution: A centralized, live-projection state container and window-event dispatcher that all layout and face components can share, with explicit guards for verification mode and continuity for the operator's lens choice.
summary_for_embedding: App.tsx is the root React orchestrator of the Surface application, managing viewport form factor, projection lens state, live event streaming, selection handoff to the V/gallery, and rendering of layout plus overlay faces.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to host every Surface face and keep the canvas, the V, and the drill-in gallery aligned on the same addressed unit and projection parameters.

symbols.does: Union type for the three classified viewport shapes.
symbols.description: Defines 'desktop' | 'portrait' | 'landscape', used by classify and useFormFactor to choose which layout component to render.

symbols.does: Union type for the circle/square/both visualization modes.
symbols.description: Defines 'both' | 'circle' | 'square', representing whether the projection shows meaning and structure together or isolated.

symbols.does: Maps width and height to a FormFactor.
symbols.description: Returns 'desktop' when width is at least 1024 and greater than height, 'portrait' when height exceeds width, and 'landscape' otherwise.

symbols.does: React hook that tracks and updates the current viewport FormFactor.
symbols.description: Initializes from window.innerWidth/innerHeight, listens to resize and orientationchange, and returns the current classified form factor.

symbols.does: Resize handler inside useFormFactor that recomputes the form factor.
symbols.description: A local callback passed to window event listeners; it calls classify with the current viewport dimensions and updates state.

symbols.does: Central type describing all state passed down to layout modules.
symbols.description: Aggregates projection data, loading/error state, lens controls (binding, embedder, space, dim, quant), selection, gallery state, motion feel, view mode, nucleation params, centre/poles, live/time/rung controls, and notice handling.

symbols.does: Type for a relative origin in the projection.
symbols.description: Holds a ref (addressable item) and a short label; used to re-project the space around an attended node.
Specifies which type registry space, which item space, and which rung drive the 'by_nucleation' lens.

symbols.does: Root Surface component that composes state, data, events, and faces.
symbols.description: Exports the main App component: it sets up form factor, projection fetching, live streaming, selection handoff, V verb dispatch, and renders the chosen layout plus all overlay faces.

symbols.does: Resize handler that re-applies modal allocation CSS variables.
symbols.description: A local callback passed to window resize/orientationchange inside the resolve-and-apply effect; triggers resolveAndApplyModal to update modal positioning.

symbols.does: Single dispatcher for gallery:verb events from the V and other consumers.
symbols.description: Branches on verb name to handle navigate (re-centre), open-source (fetch territory into SourcePanel), drive/generate notices, decision_take writes, and accept-update proposals, with verify-mode guards.
Casts window to an unknown shape to reach forkBrainCore.writeDirections, used to persist decision_take marks; falls back to a direct fetch if unavailable.
